name: Example Firewall (Blocking)
author: InSpectre Community
description: Example of a blocking-capable plugin for a generic firewall/router with a token-authenticated REST API. Demonstrates a login -> action dependency chain, bearer-token session extraction, and the required block_client / unblock_client actions. Select this plugin under Settings -> Security Responses -> Blocking Method once enabled and configured.

config_schema.label: Firewall Base URL
config_schema.help: Base URL of the firewall REST API. No trailing slash.

config_schema.label: Verify SSL Certificate
config_schema.help: Most firewalls use self-signed certificates; leave disabled unless you have installed a trusted cert.

login.description: Authenticate and obtain a bearer token. The token is stored in the session and auto-injected as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' on later calls.

block_client.description: Create a firewall rule blocking the device by MAC (and IP if known).

unblock_client.description: Remove the block rule for the device.

test_connection.description: Used by the 'Test Connection' button. Verifies credentials by logging in and reading system status.
